lookign good but can be simplified using better naming and some minor refatoring

Big step in the right direction but I still strongly recommend separate creating from appending new elements where possible (most cases!)
just return a null and check for null before append, yout create  small dedicated method for that:

void appendWhenNeeded(parent, child)

DeltaReportUtilsSpec.groovy

I guess good for further review by the team.
I see most of my comments are resolved.
Some comments from me are still not resolved. Mark them resolved once done I will review again 😊

Some major issue with the code, I ran it and the delta report generated is a mix of JSON nested in XML. Please look into this:
<deltaReports>
    <deltaReport id="1">
        <action>remove</action>
        <xpath>/bookstore</xpath>
        <source-data>
            <categories>{code=1, name=Children, books=[{lang=English, price=20, title=Matilda, authors=[Roald Dahl], editions=[1988, 2000]}, {lang=English, price=15, title=The Gruffalo, authors=[Julia Donaldson], editions=[1999]}]}</categories>
        </source-data>
    </deltaReport>
    <deltaReport id="2">
        <action>remove</action>
        <xpath>/bookstore/premises</xpath>
        <source-data>
            <addresses>{town=Maynooth, county=Kildare, street=Main Street, house-number=2}</addresses>
        </source-data>
    </deltaReport>
    <deltaReport id="3">
        <action>replace</action>
        <xpath>/bookstore/categories[@code='4']/books[@title='Debian GNU/Linux']</xpath>
        <source-data>
            <books>{editions=[2007, 2013, 2021], price=39, title=Debian GNU/Linux}</books>
        </source-data>
        <target-data>
            <books>{editions=[2007, 2013], price=39000, title=Debian GNU/Linux}</books>
        </target-data>
    </deltaReport>
    <deltaReport id="4">
        <action>create</action>
        <xpath>/bookstore</xpath>
        <target-data>
            <categories>{code=100, name=Children, books=[{lang=English, price=20, title=Matilda, authors=[Roald Dahl], editions=[1988, 2000]}, {lang=English, price=15, title=The Gruffalo, authors=[Julia Donaldson], editions=[1999]}]}</categories>
        </target-data>
    </deltaReport>
</deltaReports>
Marking this as unresolved, let me know if this is fixed
Also change the Copyright year from "2025" to "2025-2026" in all your files

I have used specific execption (DOMException,ParserConfigurationException,TransformerException)
 since these are the expected failure cases when handing XML This avoids masking unexpected runtime error.

Just looked at this code in-depth and I would not approve this approach. You are trying to convert each field of delta reprt from its java equivalent to XML and this would be really slow for real world data or deeply nested data.
Apart from that as I mentioned earlier, the use of hard coded values for id, delta report, xpath, action, source and target data can be issue from point of  maintainability.
I would like to discuss it later for alternate approach.

Not resolved, if you added annotation remove the constructors.
I think you can use @AllArgsCOsntructor instead

I tried the suggested change using @AllArgsConstructor for XmlObjectMapper.
However, after applying it, the ApplicationSpec test started failing while starting the CPS application.
The failure is due to an UnsatisfiedDependencyException.
Spring is trying to autowire XmlMapper through the generated all-args constructor, but no XmlMapper bean is defined in the application context.
Additionally, XmlMapper is not provided as a default Spring/Jackson bean.
Because of this, it must be manually instantiated unless we explicitly define it as a Spring bean.

I tried an additional approach by registering XmlMapper as a Spring bean through a dedicated configuration class.
However, the same UnsatisfiedDependencyException still occurs during CPS application startup.
I se,e its is not a bean. Then you can just instantiate in on line 32 and you annotate it with @NoArgsContructor
my comment was about xmlObjectMapperdefault  NOT BEING USED :-) I think you can simply remove it
